focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 15

The valley smelled different in the early morning. Cooler air pushed the contamination stink down close to the ground and thinned it enough that Maren could almost pretend the ozone-sweet rot was not there. Almost. The hum from the three dungeon mouths had banked low with the night, not gone quiet so much as dropped into the earth. He had slept badly on the thin bedroll Kael had lent him, the entity pressing against the inside of his skull in slow rhythmic surges like another body trying to breathe through his ribs, and when he had crawled out before sunrise the Crook had looked peaceful in the ugly way contaminated places could look peaceful. Quest-light lay in the low spots with a faint shimmer, pale as moonlight on standing water.

He was east of the camp now, on his knees in cold dirt, trying to remember the second containment pass Thessa had drilled into him the afternoon before. The ceramic bowl rested against his thigh in its loose cloth wrapping because he had carried it out with him automatically. He was not using it. He was only trying to get the order of the motions right before Thessa came looking and informed him, with complete justice, that he had forgotten half of what she had taught him overnight. His left wrist kept collapsing. His fingers kept stiffening at the wrong moment. Every time it happened he had to start again.

Behind him, the camp was waking up in pieces. Petra had the fire going, crouched low over a blackened pot with her bandaged hand wrapped around the spoon because the cold still made the knuckles seize. Her bad left hip pulled her sideways whenever she shifted her weight, but she moved around it with the old mean patience of someone who had stopped expecting the body to cooperate years ago. Errin Denna had four clay jars o...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Crook — smaller than expected, a handful of sin-eaters doing essential thankless work in contamination-heavy territory
The examination: fragments not sitting right, unclassified entity growing, contamination being redistributed rather than cleared
Thessa arguing for consumption — breaking Maren down the way quests are broken down
The supervised consumption: barely managed, but contamination decreases across a wider area than any individual sin-eater can affect
prev_tail_excerpt:
...blanket. The blister on his heel throbbed in time with his heartbeat, and the hum of the System sat under both rhythms like a third pulse.

In the morning, Derry was gone.

His side of the fire ring was neat — blanket folded, ground swept clean of crumbs. He'd left the iron pot by the cold ashes with the extra water skin propped against it. He'd taken the cheese knife, and the fire-starting kit, and nothing else.

He'd brushed out the heel marks around his bedroll too. Not enough to fool a ranger. Enough that Maren knew he'd tried.

Maren stood in the grey pre-dawn and looked at the road forking below the clearing. The left branch descended into the valley toward the Crook. The right branch disappeared into the mist, heading south toward the main road and Fenhold.

He could feel the place where Derry had been like a gap in the camp. The clearing sounded wrong without Derry talking to the fire before it was even lit.

*The iron pot,* Aldric said.

"I can see that."

Maren picked up the pot. It was cold and heavier than it looked. He slid it into his pack on top of the ceramic bowl. Iron tapped clay, then settled.

*The left fork,* Vess said. Speaking for the first time since he'd silenced her. *The Crook. Three days.*

"Yeah." Maren shouldered the pack. It rode differently now, one bedroll gone and the pot knocking low against the bowl. "Three days."

He took the left fork. The road dropped into thicker mist and stronger contamination stink, sweet and rotten at once. Quest-light burned pale in the ditches, and the right fork vanished behind him before the sun got over the ridge.

Caul had the direction now. That meant less guesswork for the ranger and less room for Maren to disappear.

Maren hitched the pack higher, felt iron knock once against ceramic, and kept walking.
secondary_character_beats: Thessa's private doubt should surface: the real sin-eating is getting harder, contamination is getting denser, and she has no one to tell. Maren's wider-area capability challenges her understanding of what sin-eating can be.
state_deltas_required: The sin-eater community examines Maren and finds him dangerous; Thessa argues for consuming him; he must demonstrate control he doesn't fully have
The community that should be his home nearly destroys him; he survives through accidental utility, not competence; Thessa begins grudgingly working with him
